verdict: Worth doing, scoped. The expert's core claim is real and verified: 62 silent-skip `return`s (52 in runtime_node.test.ts, 10 in runtime_codec_scientific.test.ts) plus 7 `expect(true).toBe(true)` asserts make Python-dependent tests pass vacuously on any machine without Python. This is the single most misleading thing in the test suite. The fix (skipIf resolved once in beforeAll) is mechanical and low-risk for the silent-skips, medium for the new real coverage. Two of the four ts-fx duplicate pairs are tests of dead code (ParallelProcessor) and should be DELETED with the module, not deduped — confirmed via grep that ParallelProcessor has no real production caller (only a no-op setDebug at tywrap.ts:48). The Pyodide pair is a genuine byte-identical duplicate. The Path Utilities pair is a near-false-positive: token-identical bodies but exercising different runtimes (Bun-native vs tywrap pathUtils), so it is cross-runtime redundancy, not a deletable dupe. I recommend doing E1/E4 now (truth-telling + dedup + dead-test deletion), and treating E2/A1/A3 new coverage as a follow-on that depends on the source fixes landing first.
verifiedSignals: E1 silent-skip: VERIFIED 62 total `if (!pythonAvailable...) return;` — 52 in test/runtime_node.test.ts (e.g. :94,:107,:119), 10 in test/runtime_codec_scientific.test.ts (e.g. :50-51). beforeAll at runtime_node.test.ts:69-79 already resolves availability but only console.warns; each `it` re-checks and returns silently.

E4 tautological asserts: VERIFIED 7 `expect(true).toBe(true)` — test/runtime_node.test.ts:98 and :626, test/generated_numpy.test.ts:13, test/generated_pydantic.test.ts:19, test/generated_scipy.test.ts:13, test/generated_torch.test.ts:13, test/transport.test.ts:734.

ts-fx dup pair ParallelProcessor #1: VERIFIED real duplicate — `should use default batch size when not specified` (parallel-processor.test.ts:208-211) and `should create instance with default options` (:17-20) both do `new ParallelProcessor(); expect(...).toBeInstanceOf(ParallelProcessor)`.

ts-fx dup pair ParallelProcessor #2: VERIFIED byte-identical — `should allow subscribing to events` (:188-192) and `should register task_complete listener` (:268-272) are identical (on('task_complete',handler); expect(listenerCount).toBe(1)).

ts-fx dup pair Pyodide: VERIFIED byte-identical — runtime_pyodide.test.ts:257-271 vs :273-287 both delete global loadPyodide, vi.doMock('pyodide') to throw, and assert rejects.toThrow('Pyodide is not available'). The :273 name claims to test 'dynamic import' but the body is identical to :257.

ParallelProcessor is dead code: VERIFIED only production reference is src/tywrap.ts:21 (import) + :48 (globalParallelProcessor.setDebug — a no-op debug toggle). No actual parallel-processing call site. Class exercised only by its own test file.
bridge-core is dead: VERIFIED grep shows only self-references in src/runtime/bridge-core.ts; test/bridge-core.test.ts is the only importer.

A1 ProcessIO restart gap: VERIFIED src/runtime/process-io.ts:533 restartProcess() calls killProcess() (:535) + resets counters (:540-541) but never rejects this.pending; pending-rejection only happens in dispose (:350-356). No test exercises restart behavior (transport.test.ts:453 only asserts the restartAfterRequests option is accepted).

A3 http id-correlation gap: VERIFIED src/runtime/http-io.ts:186-187 returns responseText verbatim with no comparison of response.id to request.id. No id-correlation assertion in test/runtime_http.test.ts.

E2 pyodide identity-mock: VERIFIED runtime_pyodide.test.ts uses a mock dispatch handler (setMockDispatchHandler at :247) — no real Pyodide round-trip; toPy/PyProxy.destroy never exercised against real Pyodide.
falsePositives: ts-fx dup pair 'Path Utilities cross-platform' (runtime_bun.test.ts:348 vs runtime_utils.test.ts:202) is a NEAR-FALSE-POSITIVE. Bodies are token-identical (same testCases table) so ts-fx matched, but runtime_bun.test.ts:338-359 runs the cases through Bun's NATIVE pathUtils (file is bun-gated) while runtime_utils.test.ts:202 runs them through tywrap's own src/utils/runtime.ts pathUtils. Different code under test + different runtime; deleting either loses coverage. Do NOT delete — at most extract the shared testCases array into a fixture.

Expert review item 'optimized-node.ts is dead, delete optimized-node.test.ts' is PARTLY wrong for the test. test/optimized-node.test.ts:5 imports NodeBridge (aliased as OptimizedNodeBridge) from src/runtime/node.js, NOT the dead src/runtime/optimized-node.ts shim. The TEST exercises live NodeBridge and must be KEPT (folded into runtime_node.test.ts), even though the optimized-node.ts shim module is deleted.
SIGNAL-DIGEST says '63 silent-skip'; actual verified count is 62 (`grep -rhE 'if \(!pythonAvailable.*\) return' test/ | wc -l` = 62). Minor, but cite 62.
proposal:
Introduce a shared test capability helper and convert all environment-gated tests from silent-skip to declarative skipIf, delete tests of soon-deleted dead code, dedupe verified duplicates, and add the three missing real-coverage suites.

1. New file test/helpers/env.ts exporting resolved-once capability flags: `pythonAvailable` (resolvePythonExecutable + --version, run once at import/module top), `bridgeScriptAvailable` (existsSync('runtime/python_bridge.py')), `pyodideInstalled` (can import 'pyodide'), and per-lib `hasPythonModule(name)`. These are evaluated once (top-level await or a shared beforeAll-seeded module singleton) so a broken Python setup surfaces as 'N tests skipped' in the reporter rather than green-but-empty.

2. runtime_node.test.ts: replace the per-`it` `const pythonAvailable = await isPythonAvailable(); if (!pythonAvailable || !isBridgeScriptAvailable()) return;` (52 sites) with `describe.skipIf(!pythonAndBridge)(...)` at the block level, or `it.skipIf(!pythonAndBridge)(...)` per test. Delete the `expect(true).toBe(true)` at :98 (assert dispose resolved / bridge state) and :626 (assert concrete outcome). Fold the surviving optimized-node.test.ts (which actually tests NodeBridge) into this file under a 'pool-config' describe.

3. runtime_codec_scientific.test.ts: same conversion for its 10 sites using `it.skipIf(!pythonAvailable || !hasPythonModule('scipy'))` etc.

4. generated_numpy/scipy/torch/pydantic.test.ts: replace the `if (!existsSync) { expect(true).toBe(true); return }` pattern with `describe.skipIf(!existsSync(generatedPath))` (the pattern generated_runtime.test.ts:18 already uses correctly — make it the standard).

5. Delete dead-code test files: test/parallel-processor.test.ts (entire), test/bridge-core.test.ts (entire), test/performance-integration.test.ts (imports dead BundleOptimizer), test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts (imports parallel-processor + bundle-optimizer + memory-profiler). The two ParallelProcessor ts-fx dup pairs die with parallel-processor.test.ts — no dedup needed.

6. Dedupe: in runtime_pyodide.test.ts delete the redundant :273-287 test ('should attempt dynamic import') OR rewrite it to actually mock a SUCCESSFUL dynamic import and assert the bridge initializes from it (the more useful fix, since the name implies a path currently untested). For Path Utilities, extract the shared cross-platform testCases array into test/fixtures/path-cases.ts; keep both call sites.

7. Add real coverage: (a) test/runtime_process_restart.test.ts — drive ProcessIO past restartAfterRequests with an in-flight request and assert it rejects with BridgeError rather than hanging (A1); (b) id-correlation tests in runtime_http.test.ts and runtime_pyodide.test.ts asserting a mismatched response.id throws BridgeProtocolError (A3); (c) a `describe.skipIf(!pyodideInstalled)` real-Pyodide round-trip block in runtime_pyodide.test.ts for Uint8Array/bytes and PyProxy.destroy accounting (E2). (b) and (c) depend on the corresponding source fixes landing.
breakingChanges: Deletes test files test/parallel-processor.test.ts, test/bridge-core.test.ts, test/performance-integration.test.ts, test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts (only valid once the delete-dead-code theme removes the corresponding src modules; deleting tests before the modules would leave dead modules untested but still present).

Tests that previously passed vacuously without Python will now report as SKIPPED (visible in reporter) instead of PASSED. CI summaries / coverage gates that counted these as passing green will see a drop in 'passed' count and a rise in 'skipped' — any CI assertion on exact pass counts must be updated.

If CI lacks Python or the bridge script, large swaths of runtime_node / scientific tests now skip rather than silently pass; teams relying on these as a green signal must ensure Python is installed in CI (it currently is per CLAUDE.md test:python suites).
Folding optimized-node.test.ts into runtime_node.test.ts removes the standalone file path (anyone referencing test/optimized-node.test.ts in scripts/filters must update).
verificationPlan: Behavior-preserving for the conversion + dedup; intentionally behavior-changing for the new coverage. Steps: (1) Before changes, run `npm test` with Python present and capture the passed/skipped counts as baseline. (2) After the skipIf conversion, run `npm test` WITH Python and confirm the same set of assertions execute (passed count unchanged for the converted tests; the deleted expect(true) lines should be replaced by real assertions that still pass). (3) Run `npm test` WITHOUT Python (e.g. PATH-stripped or TYWRAP_CODEC_PYTHON pointing nowhere) and confirm the previously-vacuous tests now report as skipped, not passed — this is the proof the truth-telling works. (4) For deleted dead-code tests, confirm via grep that no other test imports the deleted helpers and that total suite count drops by exactly the removed cases. (5) Re-run ts-fx tests/duplicate scan and confirm the ParallelProcessor and Pyodide pairs are gone (Path Utilities pair will remain by design — note it). (6) For new ProcessIO-restart/id-correlation tests, write them RED first against current source to prove they catch the bug, then GREEN after the A1/A3 source fixes land.
steps: Create test/helpers/env.ts: resolve `pythonAvailable`, `bridgeScriptAvailable`, `pyodideInstalled`, and `hasPythonModule(name)` once (module-level, cached). Export booleans/predicates for skipIf use.

Convert test/runtime_node.test.ts: replace all 52 `const pythonAvailable = await isPythonAvailable(); if (!pythonAvailable || !isBridgeScriptAvailable()) return;` sites with describe.skipIf/it.skipIf using env.ts flags. Delete the two `expect(true).toBe(true)` (:98 -> assert dispose/init resolved; :626 -> assert concrete outcome).
Convert test/runtime_codec_scientific.test.ts: replace its 10 silent-skips with it.skipIf(!pythonAvailable || !hasPythonModule('scipy'|'torch'|'sklearn')).

Convert test/generated_numpy.test.ts, generated_scipy.test.ts, generated_torch.test.ts, generated_pydantic.test.ts to describe.skipIf(!existsSync(generatedPath)) (mirror generated_runtime.test.ts:18); remove their expect(true).toBe(true).
Delete test/parallel-processor.test.ts (dead code; absorbs both ParallelProcessor ts-fx dup pairs).
Delete test/performance-integration.test.ts and test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts (import dead BundleOptimizer / ParallelProcessor / MemoryProfiler).
Fold test/optimized-node.test.ts (which tests live NodeBridge, NOT the dead shim) into test/runtime_node.test.ts under a 'pool / warmup config' describe; then delete optimized-node.test.ts.

Dedupe Pyodide: in test/runtime_pyodide.test.ts either delete :273-287 OR rewrite it to mock a SUCCESSFUL dynamic import and assert bridge init from it (preferred — covers the currently-untested success path).
Dedupe Path Utilities: extract the shared cross-platform testCases array to test/fixtures/path-cases.ts; keep both runtime_bun and runtime_utils call sites (different runtimes). Do not delete either.
Add test/runtime_process_restart.test.ts: drive ProcessIO past restartAfterRequests with an in-flight request, assert rejection (not hang). Write RED against current source, GREEN after A1 fix.
Add id-correlation tests in runtime_http.test.ts and runtime_pyodide.test.ts: feed a response whose id != request id, assert BridgeProtocolError. RED-then-GREEN with A3 fix.
Add describe.skipIf(!pyodideInstalled) real-Pyodide round-trip block (Uint8Array/bytes, PyProxy.destroy accounting) per E2.
Run full verification plan: baseline counts, with-Python run, without-Python run (prove skips), ts-fx re-scan, dead-test grep.
